data.title: Hick’s Law
data.description: Streamlined decision points are designed to reduce the user's cognitive load.
data.technical_spec: UI design minimizes complex navigation paths and opt for simple, clear options.

data.title: Fitts’s Law
data.description: Interactive elements are made accessible by considering their size and proximity.
data.technical_spec: Optimized touch targets and shortcuts for common functionalities on mobile interfaces.

data.title: Miller’s Law
data.description: Information is chunked into categories easy for users to understand and process.
data.technical_spec: Organizes feed and stories into well-defined sections and categorizations.

data.title: Jakob’s Law
data.description: Design aligns with familiar user experiences to leverage existing mental models.
data.technical_spec: UI and UX flows mirror common patterns found on major social media platforms.

data.title: Gestalt Principles
data.description: Design follows Gestalt principles to create more intuitive and aesthetically pleasing interfaces.
data.technical_spec: Uses principles like similarity and proximity to organize content and visual hierarchy.
